name: Toast Platform API
what_it_does: Restaurant technology platform API for accessing POS data including menus, orders, payments, staff, and reporting for Toast-powered restaurants.
use_cases: Pulling real-time order and sales data for restaurant analytics agents
Syncing menu information and pricing to third-party delivery platforms
Accessing labor and timesheet data for workforce management automation
Monitoring inventory levels and generating reorder recommendations
Reconciling daily revenue with accounting systems like QuickBooks
not_for: Consumer-facing ordering apps (Toast offers its own consumer apps)
best_when: An agent manages operations for restaurants running on Toast POS, particularly chains or multi-location operators.
avoid_when: Your restaurants use a different POS (Square, Clover, Lightspeed), or you're building consumer-facing restaurant discovery apps.

auth.notes: OAuth2 client credentials flow for machine-to-machine access. Restaurant partners authenticate via client_id/client_secret. Scopes are per-resource (menus, orders, labor, etc.).

pricing.free_tier_limits: Partner sandbox available for approved developers
pricing.paid_tiers: Toast Partner program required; revenue share or licensing applies
pricing.notes: API access requires joining Toast's Partner Program. Not freely available — designed for ISVs and technology partners building on the Toast ecosystem.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits vary by endpoint and partner tier; documented in partner portal

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Documentation is comprehensive for core restaurant operations APIs. OAuth2 implementation follows standards. Webhook system for real-time order events is well-documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Order-taking endpoints support idempotency. Analytics queries are naturally idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Partner program approval required before API access — can take days/weeks
Restaurant GUID required for all data queries — must be obtained per-location
Menu data can be large for complex restaurant menus — implement pagination carefully
Webhook events for orders require HTTPS endpoint with public accessibility

security_score_components.security_notes: PCI-DSS compliant for payment processing. OAuth2 with scoped permissions. Partner vetting process adds security layer. SOC2 certified.
